methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $369M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury is a Series F robotics and manufacturing software company that has raised $369M to date. The valuation universe for robotics and manufacturing includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable and yields a $480M implied valuation, applying a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical for Series F-to-subsequent-round advancement against the $369M total raised proxy. The peer-median approach is not applicable given the narrow comp set: while 11 same-sector peers exist, filtering for identical or adjacent stage companies with comparable valuation tiers produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than three) to support a reliable median benchmark.

The triangulated range is $408M–$552M, with a median of $480M, derived entirely from the stage-progression method. This narrow derivation reflects a significant analytical constraint: the robotics and manufacturing sector lacks sufficient public and private comparables at Augury's stage and scale to triangulate via traditional multiples. The key uncertainties are customer concentration, unit economics progression from Series E, and recent funding velocity relative to comparable companies. A broader dataset of same-stage robotics companies or direct revenue multiple analysis would strengthen conviction in this range.
